Details: A session on Career as a Cost Accountant was organized for the students of FYBCOM and TYBCOM in association with ICWAI. The session aimed to create awareness about the Cost and Management Accounting profession and to guide students on career opportunities, course structure, eligibility criteria, and professional growth in the field of Cost Accounting.

A Financial Literacy Drive was conducted for the students of SYBCOM on 11/01/2025. The session was led by industry experts CMA Shivaramakrishnan and CMA Krishna Sundaram. The objective of the drive was to enhance students’ understanding of financial planning, savings, investments, and responsible financial decision-making, thereby equipping them with essential financial knowledge for personal and professional life.

A 6-day Staff Development Programme (SDP) in Financial Markets – Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management was conducted from 21/09/2024 to 28/09/2024. The programme was led by Mr. Sanket Thakar and focused on enhancing participants’ understanding of financial markets, investment strategies, portfolio construction, risk assessment, and performance evaluation through practical insights and analytical approaches.

A session titled “The Inner Voice” was conducted in collaboration with the P. N. Subramanian Foundation on 08/09/2024. The session focused on self-awareness, inner reflection, and personal development, helping participants gain insights into listening to their inner voice for better decision-making and personal growth.

Seminar on QUESTIONNAIRE FRAMING AND DATA ANALYSIS. To prepare the students of SYBMS and TYBMS to undertake high level research, publish research papers and other research work, the BMS Department arranged sessions on Questionnaire Framing and Data Analysis on 26/11/2023 and 03/12/2023. Dr. Chandra Iyer, Assistant Professor, Department of Management Studies, B. K. Birla College of Arts, Science and Commerce (Empowered Autonomous) was the resource person. She guided students on how to identify variables, formulation of questions for the questionnaire, designing the layout and testing the questionnaire, coding and analysis of the collected data. Dr. Iyer conducted hands-on training on descriptive statistical analysis, percentage analysis, average score analysis, testing of hypothesis using ANOVA, correlation and regression analysis. 169 students, 2 faculties and the resource person were present for the sessions.
